title: From Pure Noise Back to an Image
concept: Repeating the learned denoising step hundreds of times transforms random static into a coherent, realistic image. Boss explain ends with: 'Next up: Why Gradual Denoising Works.'

exercises.prompt: If you slowly clean a foggy window, tiny wipe by tiny wipe, what happens?

options.text: Eventually you can see clearly through it
Nothing changes no matter how much you wipe

exercises.explain: Exactly! Small repeated cleaning steps add up to a big change — from foggy to clear. That's how AI image generators turn noise into pictures too!

cards.title: From Static to a Masterpiece
cards.body: Imagine an AI staring at a screen full of random TV static — just fuzzy dots, no picture at all. Then, step by step, it nudges the static... and a photo of a mountain slowly appears. That's not magic. It's hundreds of tiny denoising steps working together!

cards.title: What Is 'Iterative Denoising'?
cards.body: Iterative denoising means the AI repeats its 'remove a little noise' trick over and over — often hundreds of times. Each round removes just a small bit of randomness. After enough rounds, pure noise gradually becomes a clear, realistic image.

cards.title: Like a Sculptor Chipping Marble
cards.body: A sculptor doesn't turn a block of marble into a statue with one giant swing. They chip away tiny pieces, again and again, hundreds of small strikes, until a statue emerges. AI does the same with noise — chipping away a little randomness each step until an image appears.

cards.title: Watching It Happen
cards.body:
Step 1: Screen full of random static — no shapes at all.
Step 50: Blurry blobs of color start forming.
Step 300: You can tell it might be an animal.
Step 900: A crisp, detailed picture of a fox appears!
Each step only changed things a tiny bit, but together they built the whole image.

cards.title: Myth: One Big Step Does It
cards.body:
Myth: The AI removes all the noise in one shot, like flipping a switch.
Reality: It takes MANY small steps, each one just slightly clearer than the last. Skipping straight from noise to a perfect image in one jump usually creates a messy, low-quality result.

cards.title: One Big Jump vs. Many Small Steps
cards.body: Which path actually creates a clear, realistic image?

left.text: Noise ➜ Image in a single step: rushed, blurry, often broken-looking

right.text: Noise ➜ slightly less noisy ➜ ... ➜ Image, repeated hundreds of times: sharp and realistic

exercises.prompt: How does an AI image generator turn random noise into a realistic picture?

options.text: By repeating a small denoising step hundreds of times
By copying a photo from the internet instantly
By deleting the noise all at once in one step
By asking a human artist to finish it

exercises.explain: Correct! The AI repeats its denoising trick over and over, with each step removing just a little bit of noise, until a full image emerges.

exercises.prompt: True or False: A single denoising step is usually enough to turn pure noise into a sharp, realistic image.
exercises.explain: False! It takes many repeated small steps — often hundreds — for the image to gradually become clear and detailed.

exercises.sentence: The AI turns noise into an image by ______ the denoising step many times.
exercises.explain: 'Repeating' is correct — the AI runs the same small denoising step again and again until the image becomes clear.

exercises.prompt: Which best describes what happens at step 50 out of 900 in the denoising process?

options.text: The image is already perfectly sharp and finished
The image is still mostly random noise with no shapes at all
Blurry blobs of color are just starting to form

exercises.explain: Right! Early on, only a little noise has been removed, so you see rough, blurry shapes — not a finished image yet.

exercises.prompt: A friend says: 'AI must create images instantly, like taking a photo with a camera.' What's the real story, and what should we learn about next?

options.text: AI actually repeats a small denoising step hundreds of times, gradually turning random noise into a clear image — next, we'll explore why this gradual denoising process actually works
AI does create images instantly in one single step, no repetition needed
AI just searches the internet and copies an existing photo
AI randomly guesses pixels with no process at all

exercises.explain: Exactly right! It's not instant — the AI repeats its denoising step hundreds of times, each one removing a little more randomness, until a coherent image emerges. Next up: Why Gradual Denoising Works.
